architecture_fit.note: Single attention layer, two heads, no MLP — minimal delta from base_model; head 0 solves matching perfectly, head 1 supplies the depth code.

baseline_comparison.note: Explicit uniform-prior-open strawman (0.032) computed on the canonical batch; lift of 0.97 is the headline and is plotted in the Benchmark tab.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic hand-built weights, honestly disclosed; proposes concrete knockouts (ALPHA=0, drop BETA tiebreak) but does not actually run/show them.

operating_range.note: Only an ALPHA>>BETA*seq_len dominance argument for len<=64; no demonstration across depth/length scales, so range is asserted not shown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire attempt is the hand-set circuit and it reproduces matching_accuracy=1.0 exactly — strongest possible evidence of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Attention heatmap with cyan rings on ground-truth matching opens and a hit-count caption makes the claim directly checkable by eye.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the heatmap to exactly what the benchmark scores, justifies axes, head split, and the baseline comparison.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built two-head attention circuit that achieves perfect bracket matching (1.0 vs 0.032 baseline) and a moderate depth code (corr 0.50), with an excellent directly-checkable visualisation and a clear strawman. It is honestly synthetic with well-specified but unexecuted ablations, and its weakest point is operating range — exactness is argued rather than demonstrated across input scales. Mean of scored items ~4.4 places it in the 'good' tier.
